title: Canada's Mining Industry, Foreign Policy and Neoliberalism in Africa - Dissident Voice
impact_evidence: Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Anti-corruption and bribery risks -> "First Quantum stands accused of cheating Zambia out of tens of millions of dollars in taxes. title: Down the Drain? The Economic Costs of Illicit Financial Flows In Africa's Extractive Sector [Article] - GhanaStar
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "In Zambia, for example, the incidence of poverty did not change, at 60%, during 2000–10, despite a doubling of economic output." | "Zambia, the second largest producer of copper in Africa, has also registered an increase in fiscal deficits to about 10% of GDP in 2016. This is due to falling prices of copper, which contributed about 73% of total exports in 2015." | "In both countries, rents from extractive resources have failed to leverage sustainable economic growth and development." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "In Zambia over the same period a record of $28.9 billion of copper exports to Switzerland, which is more than half of all its copper exports, did not reflect in Switzerland's import statistics." | "Trade misinvoicing, which is a major aspect of illicit financial flows, is a bottleneck on Africa's growth and opportunity ... It denies African governments billions of dollars in foreign exchange and taxes revenue each year." | "It also weakens political and economic institutions that are key to state building because the underinvoicing of exports and overinvoicing of imports make it harder for concerned state institutions to impose and collect taxes and levies."
